---
title: Scripts
---

`ir run` executes R scripts whose package metadata is embedded at the top of the file.
The metadata block is YAML, with each line prefixed by `#| ` after an optional shebang.

```r
#!/usr/bin/env -S ir run
#| packages:
#|   - dplyr>=1.0
#|   - tidyr
#| r-version: ">= 4.0"
#| isolated: true
#| exclude-newer: "2024-01-15"

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

1 + 1
```

Run the file through `ir`:

```console
$ ir run script.R
$ ir run --vanilla script.R
$ echo 'print(commandArgs(TRUE))' | ir run - stdin-arg
$ ./script.R
```

## Frontmatter Metadata

The script frontmatter supports:

- `packages`: package refs resolved with pak.
- `r-version`: an optional R version requirement selected from installed versions reported by rig.
- `isolated`: `true` disables the user library for the run.
- `exclude-newer`: an optional `YYYY-MM-DD` date that resolves CRAN from the Posit Package Manager snapshot for that date. When `r-version` is omitted, `ir` also selects the newest installed R version released on or before that date.

Supported version specs include `pkg`, `pkg>=1.0`, and `pkg==1.2`.
Source refs are resolved with pak and then passed through to renv, including GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, git, URL, and local package refs in forms both tools accept.
GitHub packages should use package refs such as `github::owner/repo@ref` or `github::owner/repo/subdir@ref`, not browser URLs copied from GitHub.
Local packages may be written as `local::path`, as a bare file-system path in pak-supported forms such as `./path`, or with a package prefix such as `mypkg=local::C:/path` for Windows absolute paths.
`packages` must be a YAML sequence; each sequence entry is one package ref.
Omit `packages`, use `packages: []`, or use `packages: null` when no package refs are needed.
Unknown keys are ignored after YAML parsing, so future metadata such as `sys-reqs` can be present before `ir` supports it.

## R version selection

`r-version` and `exclude-newer` control different parts of the run.
`r-version` selects the R executable.
`exclude-newer` selects the package snapshot date.
When `r-version` is omitted, `exclude-newer` also gives `ir` enough information to choose a default R version by date.

Any run with `r-version` or `exclude-newer` uses R installs reported by rig.
`IR_RSCRIPT` is ignored for those runs.
When neither field is set, `ir` uses `IR_RSCRIPT`, rig's default R install, or `Rscript` on `PATH`, in that order.

| Metadata | R selection | If no matching R is installed |
| --- | --- | --- |
| no `r-version`, no `exclude-newer` | Use `IR_RSCRIPT`, then rig's default R, then `Rscript` on `PATH`. | The selected command must exist and be runnable. |
| `r-version` only | Use the newest installed R reported by rig that matches the `r-version` spec. | Error with a `rig install` hint for the newest matching stable R available from rig. |
| `exclude-newer` only | Use the newest installed stable R released on or before the `exclude-newer` date. | Error with a `rig install` hint for the newest stable R released on or before that date. |
| both `r-version` and `exclude-newer` | Use the newest installed R that matches `r-version`. `exclude-newer` does not make `ir` fall back to an older installed R. | Error. The install hint is limited to matching stable R releases available on or before the `exclude-newer` date. |

For example, suppose `exclude-newer: "2022-04-22"` is the date of R 4.2.0.

| Metadata | Installed R versions | Outcome |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `exclude-newer: "2022-04-22"` | 4.1 only | Use R 4.1. It is the newest installed stable R released on or before the date. |
| `exclude-newer: "2022-04-22"` | 4.3 only | Error with an install hint for R 4.2.0. R 4.3 was released after the date. |
| `r-version: ">= 4.0"` and `exclude-newer: "2022-04-22"` | 4.1 only | Use R 4.1. It matches the explicit `r-version` requirement. |
| `r-version: ">= 4.0"` and `exclude-newer: "2022-04-22"` | 4.1 and 4.3 | Use R 4.3. Explicit `r-version` selection is not capped by `exclude-newer`. |
| `r-version: ">= 4.2"` and `exclude-newer: "2022-04-22"` | 4.1 only | Error with an install hint for R 4.2.0. R 4.1 does not match `>= 4.2`. |
| `r-version: "4.3"` and `exclude-newer: "2022-04-22"` | 4.1 only | Error because R 4.3 was not available on or before the snapshot date. |

Bare `r-version` values such as `"4.2"` match installed patch releases in that series, such as 4.2.3.
Comparison specs such as `">= 4.2"` compare numeric versions.
Symbolic rig names such as `next` and `devel` are used only when explicitly requested.

## Inline runs

Inline expressions use the same resolver and cache:

```console
$ ir run -e '1 + 1'
$ ir run --with cli -e 'cli::cli_alert_success("works")'
$ ir run --with 'dplyr>=1.1' --with tidyr -e 'library(dplyr); library(tidyr); 1'
```

`--with` may be repeated and accepts comma-separated packages.
With a script, command-line packages are merged with the script metadata; with `-e`, they are the only declared packages.
Inline expressions and standard-input programs do not have frontmatter.
Without `--with`, they run against an empty resolved library while user libraries remain visible unless `--isolated` is supplied.

## Isolation

By default, `ir` prepends the resolved library to `.libPaths()` and leaves user libraries available as a fallback.
Use `--isolated` to remove the user library for the run:

```console
$ ir run --isolated script.R
$ ir run --isolated --with cli -e 'cli::cli_alert_success("hi")'
```

`--isolated` sets `R_LIBS_USER=NULL`.
Site and base/system libraries remain on the path.
